page: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 256 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It features a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA extensions. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps but does not include any local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with the server achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, ray tracing, and image processing. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the instance in the top 25% of evaluated hardware, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 337.58 GB/sec for large block read operations. The hardware also demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, alongside capable prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6i.metal offers high resource density and direct bare-metal access to the underlying Intel Xeon hardware, making it suitable for workloads requiring raw compute power without virtualization overhead. However, the lack of integrated local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This server is suited for highly parallelized compute tasks, software compilation, cryptographic processing, static web serving, and multi-threaded database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and 256 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography. While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it delivers a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in ray tracing, image processing, and Passmark CPU tests, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Single-core performance and text generation speeds fall into the average range. This bare-metal configuration is designed for high-density, multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, cryptographic operations, static web serving, and parallelized database tasks that benefit from direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for high-density compute and multi-threaded tasks.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs and high-speed networking for demanding multi-threaded and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores via dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors
256 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read/write bandwidth performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
Top-tier benchmark results in ray tracing, image processing, and cryptography
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and parallel compute tasks
